metadata.author: Joel
metadata.subject: The Day of Jehovah and the Outpouring of the Spirit of God for the Restoration of the People of God.

1: The word of Jehovah that came to Joel the son of Pethuel.
Hear this, you elders, and give ear, all inhabitants of the land. Has such a thing happened in your days or even in the days of your fathers?

What the cutting locust has left, the swarming locust has eaten; and what the swarming locust has left, the licking locust has eaten; and what the licking locust has left, the consuming locust has eaten.
2: Blow a trumpet in Zion, and sound an alarm in My holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of Jehovah is coming; for it is near.
Even now, declares Jehovah, return to Me with all your heart, and with fasting and with weeping and with mourning.
And it shall come to pass afterward that I will pour out My Spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters will prophesy, your old men will dream dreams, your young men will see visions.
3: For behold, in those days and at that time, when I return the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem, I will also gather all the nations and bring them down to the valley of Jehoshaphat; and I will enter into judgment with them there.
But Jehovah will be a shelter for His people and a stronghold for the children of Israel.

4.text: The four kinds of locusts represent the four stages of the world empires (Babylon, Medo-Persia, Greece, and Rome) that devastated Israel until the coming of Christ.

28.text: This prophecy was initially fulfilled on the day of Pentecost (Acts 2), marking the beginning of the New Testament age of the Spirit.

12.text: Jehoshaphat means 'Jehovah judges.' This valley is the site of the final judgment of the nations at the end of the age.
